page: Amazon Web Services m8i.16xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. This CPU provides 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. The memory configuration consists of 256.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 7 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieved a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score of 21499.0 and a strong multi-core stress-ng CPU performance of 69210.19. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached memory bandwidth reaching a strong 5137.47 GB/sec, and large block uncached read bandwidth at 196.85 GB/sec. However, large block uncached write bandwidth is moderate at 80.7 GB/sec. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, contribute to strong performance in cryptography, integer manipulation, and vector calculations.

This server is designed for workloads requiring balanced compute and memory resources without local storage requirements. It demonstrates top-tier performance in software compilation, static web serving throughput, and text processing. It also supports LLM inference workloads, achieving a top-tier prompt processing speed of 39.74 tokens/sec for a 70B model, though text generation for the same model drops to an average of 3.49 tokens/sec. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs makes this instance a cost-efficient choice for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and web serving that rely on network-attached storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.16xlarge is a general-purpose server utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. It features 64 vCPUs, 256.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in software compilation, image processing, and static web serving throughput, alongside strong multi-core CPU and memory read bandwidth results. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which accelerate cryptography and matrix operations. This hardware profile is suited for database operations, web serving, software development, and moderate LLM inference tasks that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.16xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier performance in software compilation and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.16xlarge is a general-purpose server with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 server featuring sixty-four vCPUs, high-speed DDR5 memory, and advanced Intel matrix extensions for compute-intensive general workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 64 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
256.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier performance in software compilation, image processing, and web serving
Advanced instruction support including Intel AMX and AVX-512
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPUs
Suited for database operations, web serving, and LLM prompt processing
